senses.definition: a group of words that have a particular meaning when used together

examples.cn: 令人难忘的/朗朗上口的短语

examples.en: ‘Start slowly’ is the key phrase for the first-time marathon runner.
examples.cn: 对初次参加马拉松比赛的运动员来说“开始阶段速度要慢”是至理名言。

examples.en: She was, in her own favourite phrase, ‘a woman without a past’.
examples.cn: 用她自己最喜欢的字眼说，她是个 “没有不清白过去的女人”。
丰富多彩的单词和短语

extra_examples.en: He just comes out with the same old stock phrases.
extra_examples.cn: 他总说那些老一套的陈词滥调。

extra_examples.en: Her unfortunate choice of phrase offended most of the audience.
extra_examples.cn: 她不恰当的措辞惹恼了大多数观众。

extra_examples.en: In 1998, he trademarked the phrase ‘Freedom of Expression’.
extra_examples.cn: 在 1998 年，他将词语“表达自由”注册为商标。

senses.definition: a small group of words (usually without a finite verb) that together have a particular meaning and that typically form part of a sentence. ‘the green car’ and ‘on Friday morning’ are phrases.
a short series of notes that form a unit within a longer passage in a piece of music
used to introduce a well-known expression that you have changed slightly in order to be funny
senses.definition_cn: （引出为逗趣而稍加改动的名言）

examples.en: Tasting is believing, to coin a phrase! (= the usual phrase is ‘seeing is believing’).
examples.cn: 品尝就是相信，铸造一句话！(=常用短语是“眼见为实”)。

senses.definition: used to show that you are aware that you are using an expression that is not new
senses.definition_cn: 套用一句老话；用老话来说

examples.en: Oh well, no news is good news, to coin a phrase.
examples.cn: 噢，常言道，没有消息就是好消息。

senses.definition: a particular way of describing something
senses.definition_cn: 措辞；表达方式；描述方式

examples.en: He is meticulous in his choice of words and turns of phrase.
